markdown_projection.note: JSON contract is authority. No markdown projection is generated for WP-KERNEL-009 (prevents projection drift).

scope.scope: Expose backend APIs for listing, reading, searching, and linking UserManual pages. This MT belongs to UserManualAndNoContextOps. Make UserManual a built-in product surface that enables no-context model and future user operation.
constraints: Use the existing microtask_plan_draft execution surface only; do not introduce a separate MT execution surface or suite field.

Core Handshake behavior must be Handshake-native: no outside app, outside server, external daemon, Docker-only path, SQLite fallback, Redis/Valkey cache authority, or sidecar paper as proof or authority.

Allowed external runtime inputs are limited to operator-provided models, GGUF/model files, tensors/safetensors, and CUI portable artifacts where the owning WP explicitly gates them; Tiptap and Monaco are allowed only as bundled/lockfile-governed product libraries integrated into Handshake.

PostgreSQL plus EventLedger is canonical for durable state, receipts, indexing evidence, runtime proof, and validation; generated markdown, wiki pages, HTML, context bundles, debug reports, and UI projections are projections only.
Every implementation handoff must be restartable by a no-context local model using only the WP/MT contract, current Master Spec bundle, product code, tests, and receipts.
The canonical stuck-together product term is UserManual; legacy ModelManual, Model Manual, and model_manual may remain only as declared deprecated shims with migration tests.
UserManual updates are build deliverables for every touched feature/tool/primitive/surface, not optional docs.
scope.expected_behavior: UserManualBackendApi leaves a concrete, restartable implementation or activation artifact with PostgreSQL/EventLedger authority, Master Spec anchors, UserManual impact, and validator-readable evidence.
acceptance_criteria: UserManualBackendApi is implemented or specified with enough detail for a no-context local model to continue from the MT alone.
The MT names exact product/governance files, state tables or APIs, constraints, failure modes, and verification evidence.
The MT rejects sidecar research-paper authority and outside-runtime proof; accepted research ideas are restated as Handshake-native requirements.
The MT handoff names HBR rows proven, blocked, or not applicable with concrete reasons.
verification: Parse the WP/MT contract and confirm this MT keeps all required fields populated.
Run the smallest relevant product, governance, or fixture test available for the touched surface; if not implemented yet, define the exact future test and negative case.
For UI/editor/UserManual surfaces, use visual debugging or declare why no GUI surface exists for this MT.
Confirm no SQLite, Docker-default, external-app, external-daemon, sidecar-paper, or mock-only proof satisfies the MT.
handoff_requirements: List files changed or intentionally left untouched.
List commands/tests run and exact pass/fail/blocker status.
List EventLedger/PostgreSQL evidence, receipt IDs, fixtures, visual-debug artifacts, and repair queues produced or expected.
List remaining risks, compatibility shims, deprecated wording, and next MTs unblocked.

lifecycle.ready_for_validation_reason: UserManual backend API is implemented and proven with real Axum/PostgreSQL route tests; adversarial review PASS.

evidence.summary: Backend UserManual routes cover page list/read/search/tools/links/freshness/resync/quickstarts/legacy bridge with typed errors and EventLedger receipts where required.
MT-201 targeted API tests passed against real PostgreSQL: pages list/read with persisted bootstrap receipt, search, tools, page links, and resync permission fail-closed gates.
KB_READY_CHECKLIST_RECEIPT emitted PASS before READY_FOR_VALIDATION transition.

Heisenberg read-only adversarial review returned PASS for MT-201. Noted non-blocking hardening gap: resync no-op success returns no explicit receipt even though API module comments say resyncs are attributable.

wp_validator_review_v2.review_method: Integration Validator multi-agent workflow (wf_0ac25815-485 + wf_98386044-70a): independent per-MT reviewer + adversarial confirm on FAIL. No ACP. Governance/repo paperwork non-blocking per operator directive (2026-06-18). Bar = good code vs MT contract + WP packet + current Master Spec v02.194, plus the WP_VALIDATOR Spec-Realism Gate.
wp_validator_review_v2.summary: MT-201 requires backend UserManual APIs for listing, reading, searching, and linking pages as a no-context bootstrap product surface with PostgreSQL/EventLedger authority. The committed code (src/api/user_manual.rs) implements a real Axum /usermanual/* router backed by a real PG store, with route-level tests passing against real managed PostgreSQL (port 5544, no SQLite/mocks) and EventLedger receipt persistence verified.
findings: src/backend/handshake_core/src/api/user_manual.rs:51-73 mounts a real /usermanual/* Axum router: pages list/read/links/projection, search, tools, features, quickstarts, freshness, access-points, legacy bridge, migration-plan, spec-enrichment, and a gated POST /usermanual/resync. Full implementations, no stubs.

Reads are anonymous-capable (bootstrap surface) but attributable: get_page (user_manual.rs:205-226) appends a KNOWLEDGE_USER_MANUAL_ENTRY_RECORDED receipt via append_read_receipt and returns the event_id; store.rs:288-297 append_manual_receipt routes through db.append_kernel_event into the real EventLedger.

Resync write gate (user_manual.rs:564-588) reuses the shared real DocumentActorKind permission enum (knowledge_document/permission.rs:15,45,59): unauthenticated/cloud_model/validator -> 403 with stable reason codes, unknown token -> 400, operator/system/local_model allowed. Fail-closed, not inferred.

Tests (tests/user_manual_api_tests.rs) are real route-level HTTP probes over a loopback Axum server against real PostgreSQL via knowledge_pg_support (port 5544, isolated schema, migrations run). receipt_exists() (lines 52-64) asserts returned receipt ids actually exist in kernel_event_ledger. user_manual_support.rs:1-5,96-118 explicitly: 'No SQLite, no mock store' and skip_if_no_pg! now PANICS rather than silently passing.

Committed green evidence logs confirm real runs: green-user-manual-api-after-review-fix.log shows 13 passed (incl. mt201_pages_list_and_read_with_bootstrap_receipt, mt201_search_finds_pages_and_tools, mt201_tools_list_and_read_resolve, mt201_page_links_resolve, mt201_resync_permission_gate_fails_closed, and mtdoc_every_registry_surface_exists_on_the_real_router) finished in 47.68s.

Spec-realism sub-rule-1 grep across src/user_manual/, src/api/user_manual.rs, and the test file found NO todo!/unimplemented!/unreachable!/deferred-live/pending-MT/Unavailable placeholder reachable from the required behavior.
sub_rule_3: lifecycle.claimed_by=KERNEL_BUILDER, completed_by=null; this is a READY_FOR_VALIDATION handoff, not a self-certified COMPLETED transition. Proof is real regardless.

Aside (non-blocking, expected per ground rules): packet/MT cite v02.192/v02.193 spec anchors while current is v02.194; the MT-124 evidence note about a resync no-op not emitting an explicit receipt is a minor hardening gap, not a behavior defect.

minimum_controls: No placeholders, mocks, scaffolding, or deferred-live escapes; runtime proof against Handshake-managed PostgreSQL/EventLedger where the contract names durable state.
